title: Coin Sum Infinite
hints: This is a coin change problem with infinite supply of each coin.
Use dynamic programming: dp[x] represents the number of ways to make sum x.
Base case: dp[0] = 1 (one way to make sum 0: use no coins).
For each coin, iterate through all sums from coin value to B.
For each sum x, add the number of ways to make (x - coin): dp[x] += dp[x - coin].
Take modulo (10^6 + 7) at each step to prevent overflow.
Iterate coins first, then sums, to ensure we count combinations (not permutations).
